The bioactive constituents of Jasminum erythrocarpum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into herbal supplements and tinctures.
